title: Idempotency, Inbox Transactions, and Effects · Runtime Contract: External Effect Dedup
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must validate stable identity and fingerprint, compose inbox and domain mutation, and preserve external-effect ambiguity; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the external effect dedup increment in a duplicate-safe Go effect state machine. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict External Effect Dedup · Theory External Effect Dedup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 4321 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; separate deterministic browser proof from race-enabled and live-broker evidence; then remove the parking binding and expose the remediation gap. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use provider idempotency, effect ledgers, reconciliation, or compensation for non-transactional external effects and state residual ambiguity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
